description:
The Company

PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.

We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.

We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.

Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

Job Summary:

This job will design, develop, and implement machine learning models and algorithms to solve complex problems. You will work closely with data scientists, software engineers, and product teams to enhance services through innovative AI/ML solutions. Your role will involve building scalable ML pipelines, ensuring data quality, and deploying models into production environments to drive business insights and improve customer experiences.

Job Description:

Essential Responsibilities:

Develop and optimize machine learning models for various applications.
Preprocess and analyze large datasets to extract meaningful insights.
Deploy ML solutions into production environments using appropriate tools and frameworks.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate ML models into products and services.
Monitor and evaluate the performance of deployed models.

Minimum Qualifications:

3+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.
Experience with ML frameworks like TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn.
Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and tools for data processing and model deployment.
Several years of experience in designing, implementing, and deploying machine learning models.

Additional Responsibilities & Preferred Qualifications:

Role and Responsibilities

Support the lead of the team in performing oversight of high-impact statistical model and AI applications in a variety of business function areas, including but not limited to fraud detection, credit underwriting, marketing analytics etc.

Conduct quantitative and qualitative model validation according to Model Risk Management Policy to identify and understand model risk issues

Collaborate with business units and model developers to remediate model issues and provide subject-matter expert opinion on model improvements

Perform model and AI risk governance related activities in line with enterprise risk framework, to ensure PayPal’s AI applications are compliant with ever evolving regulatory expectation such as Responsible AI.

Qualifications

This position requires the ability and curiosity to learn various advanced modeling methods/AI techniques, covering a broader business function. This role also requires candidate to have the capability in building effective relationship with various stakeholders including business owners, model owners, model developers and control officers. The candidate must possess excellent communication, writing and presentation skills.

An advanced degree in a quantitative field, such as statistics, mathematics, computer science or engineering essential

Advanced knowledge of statistical and machine learning models (e.g., logistic regression, time series analysis, random forests, SVMs, XGBoost, CNNs/RNNs)

Possessing advanced coding skills in dealing with big data (e.g., Scikit-learn in Python, Tensorflow, Hadoop, Spark , SQL, etc.)

Relevant Modeling experience in credit scoring, fraud detection, financial forecasting, or marketing analytics obtained either in academic or financial industry

Ability to work effectively both independently and in a team environment

Ability to communicate effectively and establish constructive relationship with stakeholders

Subsidiary:

PayPal

Travel Percent:

0

PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. When making an application directly, we will never ask you to share passwords, one-time passcodes (OTP), or verification codes. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. All communication regarding your application will come from official PayPal email domains. If you suspect fraudulent activity, please report it immediately. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.

For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.

Our Benefits:

At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer comprehensive, choice-based programs, to support all aspects of personal wellbeing—physical, emotional, and financial—delivering meaningful value where it matters most. We strive to create a flexible, balanced work culture with a holistic approach to benefits, including generous paid time off, healthcare coverage for you and your family, and resources to create financial security and support your mental health.

Who We Are:

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.

Belonging at PayPal:

Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.

Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.

We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.

description:
Wir suchen zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen KI-Spezialisten / KI Referenten (m/w/d). In dieser Rolle unterstützen Sie den fachlichen Aufbau, die Einführung und die Weiterentwicklung von KI-Anwendungen im Unternehmensumfeld. Dabei verbinden Sie technisches Know-how, organisatorische Fähigkeiten sowie beratende Tätigkeiten und wirken aktiv an der strategischen Ausrichtung und Governance von KI-Themen mit.

Ihre Aufgaben
KI-Strategie, Governance und Organisation: Sie unterstützen die Weiterentwicklung von KI-Strategie und KI-Roadmap, gestalten Governance-Strukturen und das KI-Operating-Modell mit, identifizieren KI-Potenziale und Use Cases und stehen als fachlicher Ansprechpartner für KI-Themen zur Verfügung
Daten- und Technologiebezug: Sie bringen Kenntnisse im Datenmanagement, in der Datengovernance, Datenqualität und Datensicherheit ein, agieren an der Schnittstelle zu den Datenverantwortlichen und verfügen über Grundlagen zu Datenbanken, APIs, Workflow- und Prozessoptimierung sowie Machine Learning, LLMs und Generative AI
KI-Tools, Enablement und Umsetzung: Sie unterstützen die Einführung und Nutzung von KI-Tools, bewerten geeignete Business-Anwendungen je Use Case und wirken an der Entwicklung neuer sowie der Weiterentwicklung bestehender KI-Lösungen mit
Austausch, Schulung und externe Schnittstellen: Sie fördern die Akzeptanz und Nutzung von KI-Anwendungen, organisieren und moderieren Austausch- und Netzwerkformate, führen Workshops durch, identifizieren Schulungsbedarfe und agieren als Schnittstelle zu externen Know-how-Trägern und KI-Dienstleistern
Trends, Regulierung und Branchenbezug: Sie verfolgen aktuelle KI-Trends und -Technologien und bringen Grundlagenkenntnisse zu KI-Regulierung, Compliance, Datenschutz sowie zu den Prozessen und Rahmenbedingungen der Energiewirtschaft ein
Ihr Profil
Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data & Analytics oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
Sie verfügen über fundierte Kenntnisse im KI- und Datenumfeld mit klarem Business-Bezug und besitzen ein sicheres Verständnis von KI-Technologien, Daten- und Systemzusammenhängen
Sie sind in der Lage, technische Inhalte strukturiert aufzubereiten und verständlich zu vermitteln und bringen Erfahrung in der Moderation, der Durchführung von Workshops sowie in der Wissensvermittlung mit
Sie zeichnen sich durch eine selbstständige, strukturierte und analytische Arbeitsweise sowie durch Kommunikationsstärke und ein sicheres Auftreten gegenüber unterschiedlichen Stakeholdern aus
Sie haben ein hohes Interesse an technologischen Entwicklungen im KI-Umfeld
Sie verfügen über sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mindestens C1-Niveau)

Nicht alle Punkte zu 100% erfüllt? Dann überzeugen Sie uns gerne dennoch im Rahmen Ihrer Bewerbung von Ihren Fähigkeiten! Wir freuen uns darauf, Sie kennenzulernen! Wir setzen uns für Diversität und Chancengleichheit ein und suchen Menschen, die so vielfältig sind wie unser Netzwerk. Mit spannenden Entwicklungs- und Karrieremöglichkeiten innerhalb der Thüga-Gruppe entfalten Sie Ihre Energie und Fähigkeiten und gestalten mit uns eine erfolgreiche Energie- und Wärmewende!

description:
We Are:

At Synopsys, we drive the innovations that shape the way we live and connect. Our technology is central to the Era of Pervasive Intelligence, from self-driving cars to learning machines. We lead in chip design, verification, and IP integration, empowering the creation of high-performance silicon chips and software content. Join us to transform the future through continuous technological innovation.

You Are:

You are a curious and motivated engineer with a passion for developing innovative algorithms and cutting-edge machine learning solutions. You thrive in collaborative environments, enjoy tackling complex mathematical challenges, and are eager to contribute to real-world industrial applications. Your background in engineering, computer science, mathematics, or physics has equipped you with a solid foundation in programming and analytical thinking. You are comfortable working with C++ and Python, and you have experience or a keen interest in fields such as machine learning, artificial intelligence, data mining, optimization, and uncertainty quantification.

You approach problems with creativity and rigor, seeking not just to solve them, but to create robust, scalable solutions that drive product excellence. You value continuous learning, whether it’s deepening your theoretical knowledge, mastering new tools and libraries, or collaborating with experts across disciplines. You communicate effectively, are receptive to feedback, and believe that sharing ideas leads to better outcomes. You aspire to make a measurable impact in a high-tech environment and are excited by the possibility of contributing to patentable innovations and research publications. If you’re looking for a role where your technical skills and innovative mindset can shape the future of design optimization, you’ll find your place on our team.

What You’ll Be Doing:
Designing, implementing, maintaining, testing, evaluating, benchmarking, and documenting advanced algorithms and mathematical functions for ANSYS optiSLang.
Collaborating closely with customer support teams to ensure developed algorithms are applicable and effective for industrial scenarios.
Learning and applying best practices in software development, machine learning, and optimization.
Performing bug verification, release testing, and providing support for assigned software products.
Investigating and resolving technical problems identified by QA or product support, and developing innovative solutions.
Working under the guidance of experienced development managers and lead developers, contributing to both new methods and the improvement of existing implementations.
The Impact You Will Have:
Advance the capabilities of ANSYS optiSLang, driving innovation in process integration and parametric design optimization.
Enable robust machine learning-based sensitivity analysis, optimization, and uncertainty quantification, empowering customers to solve complex engineering challenges.
Enhance the efficiency and reliability of industrial applications through state-of-the-art algorithmic developments.
Support the creation of high-performance, multi-disciplinary optimization tools, expanding the reach and impact of Synopsys solutions.
Contribute to research publications and patentable ideas, shaping the future of optimization technologies.
Facilitate the seamless integration of numerical simulation tools, ensuring scalable and flexible design workflows for customers worldwide.
What You’ll Need:
Master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Data Science, Technical Physics, or a related field.
Strong proficiency in C++ and Python programming, with experience in object-oriented development.
Background in at least one of the following: machine learning, artificial intelligence, data mining, single-objective optimization, multi-objective decision making, or uncertainty quantification.
Familiarity with Python libraries for machine learning and data analysis, such as TensorFlow, Theano, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, pandas, etc.
Theoretical knowledge of regression and classification methods, and experience with high-dimensional applications.
Who You Are:
Analytical thinker with strong problem-solving skills.
Effective communicator, able to collaborate across teams and share ideas clearly.
Quick learner, adaptable to new technologies and methodologies.
Team player, comfortable working in geographically distributed groups.
Detail-oriented, committed to quality and excellence in all deliverables.
The Team You’ll Be A Part Of:

You’ll join the ANSYS optiSLang Research and Development team, a dynamic group dedicated to advancing state-of-the-art software for machine learning-based sensitivity analysis, optimization, and uncertainty quantification. The team fosters innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, working closely with experts across engineering, mathematics, and software development disciplines to deliver impactful solutions for industrial applications.

Rewards and Benefits:

We offer a comprehensive range of benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.
descriptionHTML:
We Are:

At Synopsys, we drive the innovations that shape the way we live and connect. Our technology is central to the Era of Pervasive Intelligence, from self-driving cars to learning machines. We lead in chip design, verification, and IP integration, empowering the creation of high-performance silicon chips and software content. Join us to transform the future through continuous technological innovation.

You Are:

You are a curious and motivated engineer with a passion for developing innovative algorithms and cutting-edge machine learning solutions. You thrive in collaborative environments, enjoy tackling complex mathematical challenges, and are eager to contribute to real-world industrial applications. Your background in engineering, computer science, mathematics, or physics has equipped you with a solid foundation in programming and analytical thinking. You are comfortable working with C++ and Python, and you have experience or a keen interest in fields such as machine learning, artificial intelligence, data mining, optimization, and uncertainty quantification.

You approach problems with creativity and rigor, seeking not just to solve them, but to create robust, scalable solutions that drive product excellence. You value continuous learning, whether it’s deepening your theoretical knowledge, mastering new tools and libraries, or collaborating with experts across disciplines. You communicate effectively, are receptive to feedback, and believe that sharing ideas leads to better outcomes. You aspire to make a measurable impact in a high-tech environment and are excited by the possibility of contributing to patentable innovations and research publications. If you’re looking for a role where your technical skills and innovative mindset can shape the future of design optimization, you’ll find your place on our team.

What You’ll Be Doing:

  • Designing, implementing, maintaining, testing, evaluating, benchmarking, and documenting advanced algorithms and mathematical functions for ANSYS optiSLang.
  • Collaborating closely with customer support teams to ensure developed algorithms are applicable and effective for industrial scenarios.
  • Learning and applying best practices in software development, machine learning, and optimization.
  • Performing bug verification, release testing, and providing support for assigned software products.
  • Investigating and resolving technical problems identified by QA or product support, and developing innovative solutions.
  • Working under the guidance of experienced development managers and lead developers, contributing to both new methods and the improvement of existing implementations.

The Impact You Will Have:

  • Advance the capabilities of ANSYS optiSLang, driving innovation in process integration and parametric design optimization.
  • Enable robust machine learning-based sensitivity analysis, optimization, and uncertainty quantification, empowering customers to solve complex engineering challenges.
  • Enhance the efficiency and reliability of industrial applications through state-of-the-art algorithmic developments.
  • Support the creation of high-performance, multi-disciplinary optimization tools, expanding the reach and impact of Synopsys solutions.
  • Contribute to research publications and patentable ideas, shaping the future of optimization technologies.
  • Facilitate the seamless integration of numerical simulation tools, ensuring scalable and flexible design workflows for customers worldwide.

What You’ll Need:

  • Master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Data Science, Technical Physics, or a related field.
  • Strong proficiency in C++ and Python programming, with experience in object-oriented development.
  • Background in at least one of the following: machine learning, artificial intelligence, data mining, single-objective optimization, multi-objective decision making, or uncertainty quantification.
  • Familiarity with Python libraries for machine learning and data analysis, such as TensorFlow, Theano, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, pandas, etc.
  • Theoretical knowledge of regression and classification methods, and experience with high-dimensional applications.

Who You Are:

  • Analytical thinker with strong problem-solving skills.
  • Effective communicator, able to collaborate across teams and share ideas clearly.
  • Quick learner, adaptable to new technologies and methodologies.
  • Team player, comfortable working in geographically distributed groups.
  • Detail-oriented, committed to quality and excellence in all deliverables.

The Team You’ll Be A Part Of:

You’ll join the ANSYS optiSLang Research and Development team, a dynamic group dedicated to advancing state-of-the-art software for machine learning-based sensitivity analysis, optimization, and uncertainty quantification. The team fosters innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, working closely with experts across engineering, mathematics, and software development disciplines to deliver impactful solutions for industrial applications.

Rewards and Benefits:

We offer a comprehensive range of benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.

company: CACS Copernicus Autonomous Control Systems GmbH
description:
Your mission

Design, develop, and maintain software systems for vehicle identification and tracking using cameras

Prototype advanced algorithms in Python and implement/optimize them for real-time applications using C++ or Rust to ensure high-performance data processing

Leverage computer vision, machine learning, and signal processing methodologies to improve anomaly detection and pattern recognition in video and sensor streams

Ensure system reliability, safety, and performance under real-world conditions

Collaborate with cross-functional teams (AI/ML, robotics, embedded systems, UX) to integrate end-to-end solutions

Lead architectural decisions and enforce best practices in scalability, security, and maintainability

Stay updated with the latest advancements in computer vision, machine learning, and software development, applying innovative solutions to maintain our technological edge

Mentor junior developers and contribute to code reviews and technical guidance

Your profile

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field

5+ years of professional software development experience

Strong proficiency in languages such Rust and C/C++

Proficiency in using Python data analysis libraries (e.g., SciPy, NumPy) and version control systems (e.g., Git)

Solid understanding of distributed systems and backend architecture

Experience with real-time systems, performance optimization, and high-performance data analysis

Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)

Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail

English language proficiency

What we offer

Permanent contract with immediate start
Learning & development support (tech training, German courses, driver’s license assistance)
Free Urban Sports Club membership
Job bike via JobRad & Deutschland Ticket (incl. private use)
Flexible work setup (Berlin area office + remote)
Work on cutting-edge autonomous driving projects with leading OEMs
Flat hierarchies, high ownership, and real impact, every member has a big influence on the final product
Supportive, collaborative team culture, team members are always ready to help

About us

At Copernicus Autonomous Control Systems GmbH, a wholly owned subsidiary of Ford Motor Company, we are shaping the future of intelligent mobility through advanced autonomous driving and infrastructure-based control systems.

Building on deep expertise in automated vehicle movement, we develop cutting-edge solutions that enable autonomous valet parking and maneuvering in complex real-world environments such as parking facilities, logistics hubs, and industrial sites. Our approach focuses on shifting intelligence from the vehicle to the surrounding infrastructure—leveraging AI, computer vision, and connected systems to orchestrate safe, precise, and efficient vehicle operations.

As part of Ford’s global innovation ecosystem, we play a key role in advancing Level 4 autonomous capabilities, delivering scalable and cost-effective solutions that enhance safety, optimize space utilization, and significantly improve user experience.

Driven by a commitment to innovation, reliability, and safety, Copernicus Autonomous Control Systems GmbH is dedicated to enabling a future where vehicles move seamlessly and autonomously within smart environments—making parking and low-speed vehicle operations effortless and intelligent.

description:
Amphenol-Air LB ist Teil der Amphenol-Gruppe, einer der weltweit größten Hersteller von Verbindungstechniken. Zuverlässige Lösungen, Steckverbindungen und Verkabelungen sowie innovative Entwicklungen und präzise Fertigung mit hoher Qualität, dafür steht Amphenol-Air LB. Als Teil der international agierenden Amphenol-Gruppe mit weltweit über 190.000 Mitarbeitern garantieren wir allzeit erstklassige Kontakte.

Aufgaben

Sie treiben die digitale Transformation unseres Unternehmens mit Fokus auf den Einsatz von Künstlicher Intelligenz entlang unserer Kernprozesse – insbesondere in Engineering, Vertrieb sowie Compliance & Dokumentation.

Dabei übernehmen Sie nicht nur die strategische Steuerung, sondern setzen zentrale Lösungen auch selbst um und bauen schrittweise ein leistungsfähiges Team auf.

Ihr Ziel: Messbare Verbesserungen in Effizienz, Geschwindigkeit und Qualität durch den gezielten Einsatz von KI.

Entwicklung und Umsetzung einer KI- und Digitalisierungsstrategie mit klarem Business-Fokus
Identifikation, Priorisierung und Umsetzung konkreter Use Cases (z. B. Angebotsautomatisierung, Engineering-Copilots, Dokumentationssysteme)
Eigenständige Umsetzung von Prototypen und Pilotprojekten (Hands-on)
Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung einer unternehmensweiten Daten- und KI-Architektur (z. B. LLMs, RAG-Systeme, Automatisierung)
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Engineering, Vertrieb und Qualitätsmanagement
Steuerung externer Partner
Aufbau eines kleinen, schlagkräftigen internen Teams
Definition und Tracking von KPIs (z. B. Angebotsdauer, Entwicklungszeit, Automatisierungsgrad)
Qualifikation

Fachlich

Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Ingenieurwesen, Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder vergleichbar
Mehrjährige Erfahrung in Digitalisierung, Automatisierung oder KI-Projekten – idealerweise im industriellen Umfeld
Verständnis für technische Produkte und Engineering-Prozesse
Erfahrung im Umgang mit Daten, APIs und modernen KI-Technologien (z. B. LLMs, Machine Learning, Automatisierungstools)

Persönlich

Unternehmerisches Denken und ausgeprägte Umsetzungsstärke
Fähigkeit, strategisch zu denken und gleichzeitig operativ zu arbeiten
Kommunikationsstärke und Fähigkeit, zwischen Fachbereichen und Technik zu vermitteln
Hands-on-Mentalität: Sie setzen Dinge selbst um, nicht nur konzeptionell
Hohe Eigenverantwortung und Gestaltungswille

Was Sie mitbringen sollten (konkret)

Erfahrung mit Tools/Technologien wie:

Python (für Prototyping)

APIs & Datenintegration

Cloud-Plattformen (z. B. Azure, AWS)

LLM-Anwendungen (z. B. Chatbots, Dokumentenanalyse)

Erfahrung mit einem oder mehreren der folgenden Bereiche:

Engineering / Produktentwicklung

B2B-Vertrieb technischer Produkte

Qualitäts- oder Compliance-Prozesse

Benefits
Direkte Zusammenarbeit mit der Geschäftsführung
Hoher Gestaltungsspielraum und echte Verantwortung
Möglichkeit, eine zentrale Zukunftsfunktion im Unternehmen aufzubauen
Kurze Entscheidungswege und mittelständische Struktur
Attraktives Vergütungspaket inkl. Bonus, sowie attraktive Zusatzleistungen, z. B. arbeitgeberfinanzierte Altersvorsorge, Kinderbetreuungszuschuss und Essensgutscheine
Flexible Arbeitszeiten (Gleitzeit) für eine bessere Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Privatleben
Attraktives Vergütungspaket inkl. Bonus, sowie attraktive Zusatzleistungen, z. B. arbeitgeberfinanzierte Altersvorsorge, Kinderbetreuungszuschuss und Essensgutscheine

Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

description:
Building an AI‑first organisation is central to Clinisys’ purpose and the impact we deliver. As a global provider of intelligent diagnostic informatics solutions, we build AI‑enabled, cloud‑based platforms to enhance diagnostic workflows across healthcare, life sciences, and public health. By applying intelligent technology thoughtfully and responsibly, we help laboratories and testing environments operate more effectively, generate meaningful insights at scale, and ultimately support healthier and safer communities. Operating across more than 30 countries, Clinisys expects all colleagues—regardless of role or function — to work confidently with AI‑enabled tools, apply digital and analytical thinking, and continuously adapt as technologies evolve, must drive an AI first sense of purpose and urgency.

Clinisys has built an unrivalled reputation for deploying complex diagnostic networks and academic centres – and is the only provider to repetitively deliver to all disciplines end-to-end – at scale. Fostering healthier communities.

Role Description

The Systems Engineer is responsible for setting up Clinisys application environments and their migrations. They are an expert in their field of expertise and are the customer’s single point of contact during the intervention. In an increasingly data-driven and AI-enabled environment, the Systems Engineer also contributes to the deployment, operation, and reliability of platforms that leverage advanced analytics and AI-driven capabilities within Clinisys solutions.

Responsibilities

Follow the instructions of your manager and the project manager in the context of a new deployment.

Inform the project manager of recommendations and potential technical risks, including those related to performance, data quality, or AI-enabled components.

Prepare interventions and associated technical documentation.

Ensure that the CRM tool contains the necessary product licenses in the project and generate the key license file prior to on-site or remote installation.

Carry out assigned project tasks according to the schedule defined by the project manager, respecting estimates and ensuring customer satisfaction.

Act as the technical owner of assigned tasks, including environments hosting advanced analytics or AI-enabled services.

Support and operate application components that may include AI-driven features such as intelligent workflow optimization, decision support, or data analytics.

Customer training on-site or remotely, including explanations of AI-assisted functionalities where relevant.

Draft intervention reports for each on-site or remote intervention and transmit them to administrative services.

Communicate outcomes and key information to the project manager after each intervention.

Immediately alert the project manager or management to issues encountered, including data integrity, performance, or model-related concerns.

Ensure that project task progress, time tracking, and intervention reporting are accurately maintained in the CRM tool.

Establish and maintain a strong customer relationship with a clear service-oriented mindset.

Attend internal and customer-led meetings, with the project manager when necessary.

Participate in the drafting and maintenance of technical documentation, including documentation related to AI-enabled system behaviour and limitations.

Propose product and service improvements, including opportunities for automation, intelligent monitoring, or AI-driven optimization, to improve delivery time and product quality.

Contribute to team spirit and actively promote a customer-oriented approach.

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:

Core Technical Skills Knowledge and use of Linux (including vi).

Knowledge, use, and administration of Windows Server.

Knowledge of Oracle and SQL Server databases.

Knowledge of PROGRESS database is a plus. Experience with Structured Query Language (SQL).

Knowledge of healthcare and laboratory standards and protocols (HL7, Hprim, ASTM).

Network skills and understanding of file transfer and sharing mechanisms (Samba, CIFS, sFTP, NFS).

Ability to synthesize information and produce clear technical documentation using MS Office tools.

Good level of English and Fluent German.

Knowledge of the analytical laboratory environment and related vocabulary is a plus.

AI & Data-Oriented Competencies

General understanding of AI and machine learning concepts (e.g. predictive models, rule-based systems, intelligent automation) as applied to healthcare and diagnostic workflows.

Awareness of data quality, data integrity, and data governance principles, especially in regulated healthcare environments.

Ability to support and operate systems integrating AI-enabled components, including monitoring, performance validation, and incident analysis.

Familiarity with AI-assisted observability tools (log analysis, anomaly detection, performance monitoring) is a plus.

Understanding of ethical, regulatory, and security considerations related to AI in healthcare (explainability, traceability, privacy).

Interest in automation and intelligent tooling to improve deployment, monitoring, and operational efficiency.

Ability to collaborate effectively with data, product, and R&D teams on AI-related topics, acting as a technical bridge with customers.

Professional Skills

Respect for deadlines and targets, individually and as part of a team.

Strong analytical mindset with the ability to “think outside the box”.

Proactive attitude in proposing new ideas and continuous improvement initiatives.

Strong customer focus and communication skills.

Onboarding
As part of our onboarding process, all new employees will be required to attend / travel to the office on their first day of employment. This requirement is essential for onboarding activities, including the identity verification, completion of necessary documentation, receiving your IT equipment, introductions to key team members, and orientation to Clinisys policies and procedures.

Equal Opportunity Statement
Clinisys is committed to fostering an inclusive and respectful workplace. In accordance with the German General Equal Treatment Act (AGG), we welcome applications from all individuals regardless of gender identity, including male, female, and diverse. We want to make it clear that all qualified candidates will be equally assessed and considered for employment.

We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply and help us advance our mission to improve diagnostic workflows and public health outcomes across communities.

#LI-AD #LI-REMOTE

positionName: Associate Director of Engineering - Revenue Tech (m/f/d)
description:
Company Description

Launched in 2021 with a simple yet bold mission, Flink set out to give people back their most valuable resource: time. We didn't just join the quick commerce race, we defined its potential. By enabling customers to order from a curated selection of over 3,000 groceries and household essentials through our user-centric app, we deliver what people need in minutes. We have fundamentally shifted the retail landscape across Germany and the Netherlands, while embedding sustainability into our core through an all-electric delivery fleet, recyclable packaging, and a focus on reducing food waste.

Today, Flink has evolved from a disruptive startup into a resilient and sustainable powerhouse. In an industry defined by rapid change, we’ve mastered the balance of hyper-efficiency and massive scale. With a network of around 160 hubs, we now bring the Flink experience to more than 22 million people, making us an integral part of the urban infrastructure. This impact is driven by a dedicated team of more than 10,000 colleagues, including a fully employed rider fleet that serves as the backbone of our operations. By generating over €550m in revenue in 2025, we have proven that our model is not only desirable but financially formidable.

Having built a proven foundation, we are now shifting from establishing our presence to accelerating our impact. Backed by prestigious partners like Prosus, Mubadala, Bond, DoorDash, Btomorrow Ventures and REWE, and fueled by fresh investment, we are entering our next major growth phase. We are thinking bigger, expanding our footprint, and refining every link in our value chain.

Our culture is defined by a shared bias for action and the commitment to being 1% better every day. We take ownership, stay close to the frontline, and support one another to ensure we never let another Flinker fail. We celebrate diversity and believe that our multicultural team is our greatest competitive advantage.

Are you ready to shape the future of quick commerce with us?

Job Description

We are creating a brand-new engineering domain: Revenue Technology (RevTech). This domain will own the full commercial technology stack that powers how the items in our assortment are selected for each hub, how they are priced, how promotions and retail media campaigns are managed, and how product data flows through the organization. It sits alongside our Consumer Tech, Operations Tech, and Platform domains.

As the Associate Director of Engineering - RevTech (m/f/d), you will be the founding engineering leader of this domain. Paired with our VP of Revenue, who leads the domain from the Product side, you will shape the technical vision, hire and grow the teams, and deliver the systems that directly drive Flink's gross profit and assortment relevance. This is a rare opportunity to build a domain from the ground up inside a company with real scale, real customers, and real urgency.

What You Will Do

Lead the RevTech engineering organization. You will inherit a small group of engineers transferring from existing domains and grow it into a cohesive, high-performing group across multiple product teams — including Assortment, Master Data & ERP, Pricing & Promotions, and Commercial Back-office.

Partner with the VP of Revenue to own the domain roadmap. Together you will prioritize across teams, balance build-vs-buy decisions, and ensure the engineering investment maps tightly to commercial outcomes like use-case coverage, GP per category, and pricing automation.

Hire and develop engineering managers and engineers. Several key roles, including an EM for Pricing & Promotions, backend and frontend engineers, and a data scientist, are open and waiting for you to shape and fill. You will also manage the relationship with an external development partner building some of our systems, ensuring quality, architecture alignment, and a clean path to internalization.

Drive technical excellence in a greenfield-meets-brownfield context. Some systems are being built from scratch; others are being transferred and evolved. You will define the technical strategy that brings coherence to this landscape — shared APIs, data contracts, service boundaries, and on-call practices across small but critical teams.

Navigate a matrix organization. Engineers functionally report into the CTO organization while being embedded in the RevTech domain. You will work closely with fellow Engineering Directors in Consumer Tech, Operations Tech, and Platform to maintain cross-domain standards, coordinate shared services, and ensure a consistent engineering culture.

Champion AI and automation across the commercial stack. RevTech's core mission is to optimize processes via intelligent systems — from data-driven assortment engines and automated pricing to product data enrichment and demand forecasting. You will identify where AI and machine learning can create step-change improvements, partner with our Data Science team to bring models into production, and foster a culture where the team continuously leverages AI to multiply its impact.

Represent engineering in commercial conversations. RevTech sits at the intersection of technology, category management, and revenue strategy. You'll regularly engage with non-technical stakeholders — Revenue, Marketing, Supply Chain — and translate their challenges into well-scoped engineering problems.

Qualifications
An experienced engineering leader. You have 8+ years in software engineering with at least 3–4 years leading teams of teams (engineering managers reporting to you). You've built or significantly scaled an engineering organization before, ideally in a product-driven company.
Excited about AI and data-driven systems. You have a strong point of view on how AI, ML, and modern automation can transform commercial operations. Whether it's applying LLMs to product data enrichment, building recommendation engines for assortment, or using predictive models for pricing, you know how to move from experiment to production and are eager to push the boundary.
A domain builder. You have experience standing up new teams or domains — not just inheriting established ones. You're energized by ambiguity, comfortable defining scope and structure, and skilled at earning trust across functions quickly.
Technically credible. You don't need to write code daily, but you can review architecture decisions, challenge technical trade-offs, and hold your own in discussions about system design, data modeling, API contracts, and service reliability. Experience with backend-heavy systems and system integrations (e.g., pricing engines, data pipelines, ERP integrations) is a strong plus.
Commercially minded. You understand that engineering in this domain exists to move business metrics. You're comfortable working with concepts like gross profit, margin optimization, assortment logic, and retail media, or excited to learn them fast.
A strong communicator and collaborator. You will operate at the seam between engineering, product, and business leadership. You need to be equally effective in a technical deep-dive, a tech strategy conversation with the CTO, and a roadmap review with the VP of Revenue.
Pragmatic about vendor management. You have experience working with external development agencies or outsourced teams, and you know how to maintain quality and architectural coherence when not all code is written in-house.
Additional Information

What We Offer

A leadership role with real ownership: you're not maintaining a domain, you're creating one
A collaborative, low-ego leadership team that values craft and speed
A €1000 annual L&D budget as well as individual coaching options to ensure you have plenty of opportunities to learn, grow and achieve your goals
26 days of vacation, +1 day every year up to a maximum of 30 days
A mobility budget of 35 EUR per month for Deutschland Ticket subsidy
A discount on your Urban Sports Club membership
Attractive company pension options
Unlimited access to an e-learning and development platform, MyAcademy, including online German courses
Online discounts with Corporate Benefits and Future Bens
A cool discount off your personal Flink orders; be the first to test out new products!
A modern and dog-friendly office in the heart of Berlin - lots of delicious lunch spots available within short walking distance

It is our commitment that every applicant will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion.

description:
WOFÜR WIR SIE SUCHEN
Einführung von Methoden und Tools des Data Engineerings für unsere Produkt-Management-Datenbanken im Rahmen des entwicklungszentrierten Product-Lifecycle-Managements (PLM)
Entwicklung von intelligenten, skalierbaren, wiederverwendbaren und zuverlässigen Lösungen und Methoden für die Datenanalyse, -integration, -bereinigung und -verarbeitung
Generierung von neuen Insights zusammen mit unseren Domänen- und Datenexperten (m/w/d) unserer Entwicklungsbereiche und Partner (m/w/d)
Kategorie-Entwicklung, um diese mithilfe von Clustering- und Klassifikationsalgorithmen in informativere Datenstrukturen zu überführen
Aufbau von Machine-Learning Modellen, insbesondere im Bereich Clustering, Klassifikation und Textanalyse
Definition und Umsetzung von Anforderungen im Rahmen der Datenhaltungs- und Verarbeitungssysteme
Unterstützung bei der automatischen Engineering-Datenbereitstellung für die Anwendungsfelder Collaborative Engineering und digitale Verwaltung von Produkt-Entwicklungsdaten

Die Stelle kann an den Standorten Kassel, Kiel, Unterlüß oder Hamburg besetzt werden.

WAS SIE MITBRINGEN SOLLTEN
Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium, z. B. Informatik, Ingenieurwesen, Data Science oder Mathematik, oder aber eine vergleichbare Qualifikation mit den Schwerpunkten Data Science, Data Analytics
Fundierte Erfahrungen zu Methoden der semantischen Analyse (z. B. Natural Language Processing) zur strukturierten Auswertung textbasierter Daten
Gute Kenntnisse in Datenbankabfragen und Automation mit SQL (PowerShell, Skripte, PowerBI), Datenvisualisierung und Data Queries sowie objektorientierter Softwareentwicklung
Berufserfahrung im Umgang mit PLM Datenbanken und Datenmodellen von Vorteil
Kenntnisse in NLP-Frameworks (z. B. spaCy, NLTK, transformers) sowie ML-Bibliotheken wie scikit-learn oder PyTorch / TensorFlow wünschenswert
Hohe Bereitschaft, komplexe Datenmodelle zu verstehen, semantische Beziehungen zu erfassen und domänenspezifisches Wissen analytisch aufzubereiten
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Kommunikationsstärke sowie Teamfähigkeit

WAS WIR IHNEN BIETEN

An unserem Standort in Kassel bieten wir Ihnen:

Arbeiten an Gesamtsystemen
Betriebliche Altersvorsorge
Mitarbeiteraktienkaufprogramm
Ergebnisbeteiligung
Flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle, Gleitzeitmodell sowie VIVA-Familien-Service
Subventioniertes Mittagessen und Kiosk am Standort
Fitness- und Gesundheitsangebote
Corporate Benefits Plattform mit exklusiven Mitarbeiterrabatten für zahlreiche Shops
Vergünstigtes Jobticket
Individuelle und vielfältige externe sowie interne Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten, u.a. in der Rheinmetall Academy
Professioneller Einarbeitungsprozess begleitet durch ein digitales Onboarding-Tool

Mehr über unseren Standort und unsere Mitarbeiter (m/w/d) in Kassel erfahren Sie hier.

KONTAKTDETAILS

Zuständige/r Ansprechpartner/in: Sherin Schümchen

Für Fragen zu Ihrer Bewerbung, nutzen Sie bitte das Kontaktformular.

positionName: Research Engineer Neuro-Symbolic AI & Mulitmodal Assistant Systems (f/m/div.)
description:
Company Description

At Bosch, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: We grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Welcome to Bosch.

The Robert Bosch GmbH is looking forward to your application!

Job Description

Are you passionate about building intelligent systems that truly understand and reason, especially within the complex fields of manufacturing, product development, and maintenance? Neuro-Symbolic AI is leading the next big step in AI, offering huge potential to improve production, boost automation, and enable smarter decisions, bringing us closer to human-like intelligence. We are seeking a highly motivated research engineer to strengthen our team, drive the development of Neuro-Symbolic AI approaches, and enable their practical application in concrete use cases.

As part of our advanced research team, you will conduct excellent research on Neuro-Symbolic AI methodologies to combine formal knowledge representations with machine learning techniques.
You will independently develop and integrate multi-modal data pipelines for Neuro-Symbolic AI.
Additionally, you will design and implement the architecture for advanced analysis and reasoning engines for solving complex problems.
You will design and implement knowledge-based AI systems with natural language interaction.
Furthermore, you will work closely with an international team of experts to effectively transfer research solutions into business-relevant use cases such as product development, maintenance, and repair.
Last but not least, you will proactively stay informed about the latest advances in AI and machine learning to keep Bosch at the forefront of innovation in this field.

Qualifications

Education: PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field
Experience and Know-how:
expertise in Neuro-Symbolic AI architectures and frameworks
proven ability to design, implement, and integrate hybrid AI systems that combine machine learning with symbolic reasoning (e.g., knowledge graphs, rule sets, logic programming, ontologies) to meet complex requirements
advanced knowledge in building robust data pipelines for multi-modal integration (integration, cleaning, and preprocessing of heterogeneous data sources)
practical experience in applying state-of-the-art NLP techniques (e.g., Transformers, LLMs, information extraction) to understand user queries, extract insights from texts, and contribute to the automatic construction and expansion of dynamic knowledge bases
Personality and Working Practice: you succeed in translating complex research findings into practical solutions and enjoy working in an international team.
Languages: very good English skills written and spoken, German is a plus

Additional Information

https://www.bosch-ai.com
www.bosch.com/research

Please submit all relevant documents (CV, certificates, and links to GitHub or kaggle account).

We offer flexible working models: from various part-time options to mobile working and job sharing. Feel free to contact us.

Diversity and inclusion are not just trends for us but are firmly anchored in our corporate culture. Therefore, we welcome all applications, regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, ethnic origin or sexual identity.

Need support during your application?
Kathrin Stipak (Human Resources)
+49 711 811 38015

Need further information about the job?
Mirjam Steger (Functional Department)

Work #LikeABosch starts here: Apply now!

descriptionHTML:
WOFÜR WIR SIE SUCHEN
  • Einführung von Methoden und Tools des Data Engineerings für unsere Produkt-Management-Datenbanken im Rahmen des entwicklungszentrierten Product-Lifecycle-Managements (PLM)
  • Entwicklung von intelligenten, skalierbaren, wiederverwendbaren und zuverlässigen Lösungen und Methoden für die Datenanalyse, -integration, -bereinigung und -verarbeitung
  • Generierung von neuen Insights zusammen mit unseren Domänen- und Datenexperten (m/w/d) unserer Entwicklungsbereiche und Partner (m/w/d)
  • Kategorie-Entwicklung, um diese mithilfe von Clustering- und Klassifikationsalgorithmen in informativere Datenstrukturen zu überführen
  • Aufbau von Machine-Learning Modellen, insbesondere im Bereich Clustering, Klassifikation und Textanalyse
  • Definition und Umsetzung von Anforderungen im Rahmen der Datenhaltungs- und Verarbeitungssysteme
  • Unterstützung bei der automatischen Engineering-Datenbereitstellung für die Anwendungsfelder Collaborative Engineering und digitale Verwaltung von Produkt-Entwicklungsdaten

Die Stelle kann an den Standorten Kassel, Kiel, Unterlüß oder Hamburg besetzt werden.


WAS SIE MITBRINGEN SOLLTEN
  • Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium, z. B. Informatik, Ingenieurwesen, Data Science oder Mathematik, oder aber eine vergleichbare Qualifikation mit den Schwerpunkten Data Science, Data Analytics
  • Fundierte Erfahrungen zu Methoden der semantischen Analyse (z. B. Natural Language Processing) zur strukturierten Auswertung textbasierter Daten
  • Gute Kenntnisse in Datenbankabfragen und Automation mit SQL (PowerShell, Skripte, PowerBI), Datenvisualisierung und Data Queries sowie objektorientierter Softwareentwicklung
  • Berufserfahrung im Umgang mit PLM Datenbanken und Datenmodellen von Vorteil
  • Kenntnisse in NLP-Frameworks (z. B. spaCy, NLTK, transformers) sowie ML-Bibliotheken wie scikit-learn oder PyTorch / TensorFlow wünschenswert
  • Hohe Bereitschaft, komplexe Datenmodelle zu verstehen, semantische Beziehungen zu erfassen und domänenspezifisches Wissen analytisch aufzubereiten
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
  • Kommunikationsstärke sowie Teamfähigkeit

WAS WIR IHNEN BIETEN

An unserem Standort in Kassel bieten wir Ihnen:

  • Arbeiten an Gesamtsystemen
  • Betriebliche Altersvorsorge
  • Mitarbeiteraktienkaufprogramm
  • Ergebnisbeteiligung
  • Flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle, Gleitzeitmodell sowie VIVA-Familien-Service
  • Subventioniertes Mittagessen und Kiosk am Standort
  • Fitness- und Gesundheitsangebote
  • Corporate Benefits Plattform mit exklusiven Mitarbeiterrabatten für zahlreiche Shops
  • Vergünstigtes Jobticket
  • Individuelle und vielfältige externe sowie interne Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten, u.a. in der Rheinmetall Academy
  • Professioneller Einarbeitungsprozess begleitet durch ein digitales Onboarding-Tool

Mehr über unseren Standort und unsere Mitarbeiter (m/w/d) in Kassel erfahren Sie hier.

description:
Über dev-threads GmbH
Was entsteht, wenn Leidenschaft und fundiertes Fachwissen zusammentreffen? Wir gestalten die Zukunft der Softwareentwicklung, indem wir durchdachte und nachvollziehbare Lösungen für komplexe Herausforderungen schaffen. Du suchst eine Position, in der du deine Expertise in der Entwicklung anspruchsvoller Applikationen und Algorithmen einbringen kannst? Hier findest du die Möglichkeit, deine Fähigkeiten zu entfalten und einen echten Beitrag zu leisten, der dich persönlich und fachlich voranbringt.
Was bieten wir Ihnen?

Attraktive Projekte: Sie arbeiten an anspruchsvollen und abwechslungsreichen Projekten in Zukunftsfeldern wie Computergrafik, Machine Learning, AR/VR und Datenanalyse
Gestaltungsspielraum: Sie haben einen hohen Gestaltungsspielraum und die Möglichkeit, eigene Ideen aktiv einzubringen
Technologische Vielfalt: Sie arbeiten mit modernen Technologien und vielfältigen Tech-Stacks, darunter Python, C++, Web und DevOps
Teamkultur: Sie profitieren von flachen Hierarchien und direkter Kommunikation im Team
Flexibilität und Innovation: Sie genießen eine flexible Arbeitsweise in einem innovativen, entwicklungsorientierten Umfeld
Weiterentwicklung: Sie haben die Möglichkeit zur kontinuierlichen fachlichen Weiterentwicklung und Zugang zu neuen Technologien

Was erwartet Sie?

Sie entwickeln individuelle Softwarelösungen und Applikationen für Kundenprojekte
Sie setzen komplexe Algorithmen um und optimieren datengetriebene Anwendungen
Sie arbeiten an Projekten im Bereich Computergrafik, 3D, Computer Vision, Webanwendungen und moderner Softwarearchitekturen mit
Sie entwickeln Prototypen „from scratch“ und verbessern bestehende Systeme weiter
Sie analysieren technische Anforderungen und konzipieren geeignete Lösungen
Sie arbeiten in kleinen, hochqualifizierten Entwicklerteams, teilweise mit wissenschaftlichem Background, zusammen

Was sollten Sie mitbringen?
Sie haben ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium der Informatik (Master, Universität) oder vergleichbar
Sie verfügen über sehr gute Programmierkenntnisse, insbesondere in Python, und optional in TypeScript, React oder vergleichbaren Frontend-Technologien
Sie besitzen Kenntnisse im Bereich Computergrafik, 3D-Modellierung und visuelle Datenverarbeitung, wie Rendering, Geometrieverarbeitung, AR/VR
Sie haben ein fundiertes Verständnis von Algorithmen, Datenstrukturen und objektorientierter Programmierung
Sie haben Erfahrung im Umgang mit modernen Technologien und Tools, wie Git, Docker, CI/CD, von Vorteil
Sie verfügen über analytisches Denkvermögen, ausgeprägtes technisches Verständnis und die Fähigkeit, sich schnell in neue Themengebiete einzuarbeiten

Unser Jobangebot Softwareentwickler - Python / 3D / Computer Vision (m/w/d) klingt vielversprechend?

Bei unserem Partner Workwise ist eine Bewerbung für diesen Job in nur wenigen Minuten und ohne Anschreiben möglich. Anschließend kann der Status der Bewerbung live verfolgt werden. Wir freuen uns auf eine Bewerbung über Workwise.

description:
Als primärer Digitalisierungspartner der Bundeswehr erbringen wir stabile, sichere und effiziente IT-Services im In- und Ausland, vom Grundbetrieb bis in den einsatznahen Bereich und tragen so zur kontinuierlichen Erhöhung der Führungs- und Einsatzfähigkeit der Bundeswehr bei. Mit über 8.000 Kolleg*innen betreiben und modernisieren wir eine der größten und komplexesten IT-Infrastrukturen in Deutschland. Wir suchen verantwortungsvolle IT-Spezialist*innen, die die Bundeswehr-IT aus Überzeugung in herausfordernden Digitalisierungsvorhaben weiterentwickeln und so einen Beitrag zur Sicherheit Deutschlands leisten wollen.

Sorge gemeinsam mit uns für die digitale Zukunftsfähigkeit unseres Landes.

Lead IT Architect - AIOps Plattform (m/w/d)

ab sofort und in Vollzeit bundesweit an einem unserer BWI Standorte.

Werde Teil des Teams Automation & Engineering! Wir verantworten die Architektur moderner System- und Service-Management-Applikationen. Durch das Management unserer komplexen Applikationslandschaften unterstützen wir unsere Fachbereiche mit intelligenter Automatisierung.

Deine Aufgaben:

Übernahme der architektonischen Verantwortung für das Event Management System sowie den strategischen und technologischen Aufbau unserer AIOps-Plattform

Strategische Konzeption von Maßnahmen zur Weiterentwicklung bestehender Strukturen sowie Steuerung der Migration und Integration auf die Plattform

Bewertung und Priorisierung komplexer Anforderungen aus Fachbereichen oder Projekten und Erstellung von Lösungskonzepten

Federführende Erstellung und Qualitätssicherung technischer Konzepte (Architektur-, Implementierungs-, Sicherheits- und Migrationskonzepte)

Mitwirkung bei Projekten im Kontext von AIOps und Automatisierung

Dein Profil:
Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium mit Informatikbezug oder vergleichbar mit mind. 7 Jahre relevanter Berufserfahrung in der IT-Architektur
Tiefgreifende Produktkenntnisse zu IBM Netcool, Cloud Pak for AIOps oder vergleichbaren Enterprise-Lösungen
Expertise im Bereich AIOps, Event Management sowie in der Anwendung von Machine Learning zur IT-Betriebsautomatisierung.
Nachweisbare Erfahrung in der fachlichen Steuerung von interdisziplinären Entwicklungsteams und externen Dienstleistenden in komplexen Projektumgebungen
Strategisches Denkvermögen, Durchsetzungsstärke sowie die Fähigkeit, komplexe Sachverhalte zielgruppengerecht auf Management-Ebene zu moderieren

Fließende Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

Wir bieten:

Durch abwechslungsreiche und gesellschaftlich relevante Aufgaben gewährleisten wir den reibungslosen IT-Betrieb und die Digitalisierung der Bundeswehr

Das Ziel eint uns. Dabei sind für uns ein wertschätzender Umgang miteinander sowie ein großer Teamgeist elementar

Die BWI bietet eine marktgerechte Vergütung, einen sicheren Arbeitsplatz sowie eine prämierte und arbeitgeberfinanzierte betriebliche Altersvorsorge

Du startest grundsätzlich mit 30 Tagen Jahresurlaub mit Option auf individuelle Anpassungen

Über unsere Benefit-App stehen Dir ein monatliches Guthaben und Steuervergünstigungen auf Tickets für den ÖPNV zur Verfügung

Haben wir dein Interesse geweckt?

Dann bewirb dich hier

Bei Fragen steht dir das Recruiting-Team unter Angabe der Stellen-ID: 65167 gerne zur Verfügung. Tel.: 02225 988 25000

descriptionHTML:
WOFÜR WIR SIE SUCHEN
  • Einführung von Methoden und Tools des Data Engineerings für unsere Produkt-Management-Datenbanken im Rahmen des entwicklungszentrierten Product-Lifecycle-Managements (PLM)
  • Entwicklung von intelligenten, skalierbaren, wiederverwendbaren und zuverlässigen Lösungen und Methoden für die Datenanalyse, -integration, -bereinigung und -verarbeitung
  • Generierung von neuen Insights zusammen mit unseren Domänen- und Datenexperten (m/w/d) unserer Entwicklungsbereiche und Partner (m/w/d)
  • Kategorie-Entwicklung, um diese mithilfe von Clustering- und Klassifikationsalgorithmen in informativere Datenstrukturen zu überführen
  • Aufbau von Machine-Learning Modellen, insbesondere im Bereich Clustering, Klassifikation und Textanalyse
  • Definition und Umsetzung von Anforderungen im Rahmen der Datenhaltungs- und Verarbeitungssysteme
  • Unterstützung bei der automatischen Engineering-Datenbereitstellung für die Anwendungsfelder Collaborative Engineering und digitale Verwaltung von Produkt-Entwicklungsdaten

Die Stelle kann an den Standorten Kassel, Kiel, Unterlüß oder Hamburg besetzt werden.


WAS SIE MITBRINGEN SOLLTEN
  • Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium, z. B. Informatik, Ingenieurwesen, Data Science oder Mathematik, oder aber eine vergleichbare Qualifikation mit den Schwerpunkten Data Science, Data Analytics
  • Fundierte Erfahrungen zu Methoden der semantischen Analyse (z. B. Natural Language Processing) zur strukturierten Auswertung textbasierter Daten
  • Gute Kenntnisse in Datenbankabfragen und Automation mit SQL (PowerShell, Skripte, PowerBI), Datenvisualisierung und Data Queries sowie objektorientierter Softwareentwicklung
  • Berufserfahrung im Umgang mit PLM Datenbanken und Datenmodellen von Vorteil
  • Kenntnisse in NLP-Frameworks (z. B. spaCy, NLTK, transformers) sowie ML-Bibliotheken wie scikit-learn oder PyTorch / TensorFlow wünschenswert
  • Hohe Bereitschaft, komplexe Datenmodelle zu verstehen, semantische Beziehungen zu erfassen und domänenspezifisches Wissen analytisch aufzubereiten
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
  • Kommunikationsstärke sowie Teamfähigkeit

WAS WIR IHNEN BIETEN

An unserem Standort in Kassel bieten wir Ihnen:

  • Arbeiten an Gesamtsystemen
  • Betriebliche Altersvorsorge
  • Mitarbeiteraktienkaufprogramm
  • Ergebnisbeteiligung
  • Flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle, Gleitzeitmodell sowie VIVA-Familien-Service
  • Subventioniertes Mittagessen und Kiosk am Standort
  • Fitness- und Gesundheitsangebote
  • Corporate Benefits Plattform mit exklusiven Mitarbeiterrabatten für zahlreiche Shops
  • Vergünstigtes Jobticket
  • Individuelle und vielfältige externe sowie interne Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten, u.a. in der Rheinmetall Academy
  • Professioneller Einarbeitungsprozess begleitet durch ein digitales Onboarding-Tool

Mehr über unseren Standort und unsere Mitarbeiter (m/w/d) in Kassel erfahren Sie hier.

description:
Frankfurt am Main
Full-time
Permanent employee
Your Role
As part of the Growth business, the Analytics team at 360T is at the forefront of our data strategy, envisioning and executing initiatives to unlock the potential of big data and analytics across the organisation. Our global team, consisting of data strategists, analysts & quants, collaborate with data engineers to harness technology, innovation, and modern data practices. Together, we deliver a new generation of self-service tools, commercial data products and analytics services to our global client base and business stakeholders.

As a Data Analyst (f/m/d), you will be the bridge connecting business, technology, and data. You recognize data as a catalyst for innovation and digital transformation. In this role, you will collaborate with partner teams across the firm, engage with C-suite stakeholders and interact with clients tackle complex business data challenges. Your ability to create compelling visual storytelling will be crucial in turning data into actionable business value.
Your Responsibilities
Data Visualization: create impactful data visualizations using Tableau, Jupyter Notebooks and Plotly Dash to effectively communicate findings to stakeholders
Insights & Recommendations: generate actionable insights from data analysis and present recommendations to improve business performance
Data Automation: assist in optimizing internal data processes and workflows
Data Collaboration & Empowerment: work closely with cross-functional teams to support ongoing data needs of internal partners & stakeholders, clients and regulatory bodies
Stakeholder Management: build effective working relationships with key business stakeholders including C-suite executives, data engineers and other partners across the firm to achieve common data-driven goals and targets
Continuous Improvement: stay up to date with the latest trends and best practices in data analysis and visualization, and implement them to enhance 360T’s data capabilities
Your Profile
Around 3 years’ experience as a data analyst in a buy or sell-side environment
Provide a portfolio showcasing previous data visualization projects and analysis work
Passionate about data: you understand the value of data and the benefits and commercial opportunities it offers
Experience of business intelligence techniques including data modeling, data collection, data mining, data analysis, data visualization and best practices
Self-starter: be eager to learn and have the drive and will to succeed
Excellent communication and data-storytelling skills: use data to tell a story to both technical and business audiences
Proficiency in data visualization tools or libraries such as Tableau, Power BI, Plotly Dash, D3, or equivalent
Strong SQL skills
Familiarity with programming languages such as Python or JavaScript
Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, or a related field
An understanding of how the Foreign Exchange market operates
Nice to have: some experience working in a highly regulated environment and associated processes
Nice to have: experience of big data technologies (e.g. Hadoop, Spark) and machine learning
Our Offer
Competitive Salary
Bonus scheme on a quarterly basis
Contingent of flexible home office days
360T Academy for continuous professional development & training
Various career growth opportunities
Frankfurt office located directly in the city center
Regular social gatherings & company events
How to Apply
If your background and qualifications meet these specifications, please forward your application including your salary expectation, earliest starting date by clicking the “Apply” button.
Contact
Irune Del Buey
People & Culture Manager

Send email

Grüneburgweg 16-18
60322 Frankfurt am Main

#LI-DNI
About us

360T is one of the globally leading trading platforms for Foreign Exchange (FX). As Deutsche Börse Group’s powerhouse for FX, 360T provides a web-based trading technology for over-the-counter (OTC) instruments, integration solutions and related services. Since its inception in 2000, the company has developed and maintained a state-of-the-art multi-bank portal for foreign exchange, crypto assets, cash and money market products. With over 3,000 Buy-Side customers and more than 200 liquidity providers across 80 different countries, 360T is uniquely positioned to connect the global FX and Crypto industry.

Headquartered in Frankfurt am Main, Germany, 360T maintains subsidiaries in London (360 Trading Networks UK Limited), New York (360 Trading Networks Inc), Singapore (360T Asia Pacific Pte. Ltd.), Mumbai (ThreeSixty Trading Networks (India) Pvt Ltd), Kuala Lumpur (360 Trading Networks Sdn Bhd) and Dubai (360 Trading Networks (DIFC) Limited).

description:
Wer wir sind
Mit rund 6.000 Kolleginnen und Kollegen ist die GMH Gruppe das klimafreundlichste Stahlunternehmen Deutschlands. Bis 2039 liefern wir CO2-neutralen Stahl an unsere Kunden, die vor allem aus den Bereichen Mobilität, Energie und Maschinenbau stammen.
Wir setzen auf partnerschaftliche Zusammenarbeit mit flexiblen Strukturen, flachen Hierarchien und einem ausgeprägten Wir-Gefühl.

Als IT-Dienstleister der GMH Gruppe mit 20 mittelständischen Produktionsunternehmen aus der Stahl-, Schmiede- und Gussindustrie, die rund 6.000 Experten beschäftigt, sowie weiteren namhaften Kunden außerhalb der GMH Gruppe bietet die GMH Systems GmbH ein breites Service-Portfolio, wozu Prozessberatung, IT-Lösungen und Betrieb der Infrastruktur gehören.

Was wir vorhaben
Mitarbeit in unserem jungen, engagierten und kreativen Team Digital Lab
Schwerpunkt: Projekte zum Thema KI (Fokus Machine Learning) und Digitalisierung
Identifizierung von digitalen Verbesserungspotenzialen in Geschäftsprozessen mittels KI
Analyse und Aufnahme von Kundenanforderungen im KI-Kontext
Unterstützung im Data Management und Datenqualitätssicherung als Basis für den erfolgreichen KI-Einsatz
Recherche, Konzeption und Umsetzung von neuen KI-Technologien
Wen wir suchen
Abgeschlossenes Studium der Informatik / Wirtschaftsinformatik mit Schwerpunkt Machine Learning oder vergleichbare Qualifikation
Exzellente Kenntnisse im Umfeld von KI-Technologien (Fokus Machine Learning)
Kenntnisse im Bereich generativer KI wünschenswert, aber nicht zwingend
Tech Stack: Azure, Python, Git, REST APIs, JSON, etc.
Interesse an den Produkten & Prozessen der metallverarbeitenden Industrie
Lernbereitschaft und Begeisterung für neue Entwicklungen von Data & AI Technologien
Soft Skills: Freude an der direkten Zusammenarbeit mit Kunden und im Team, Eigeninitiative und eine strukturierte, zielorientierte Arbeitsweise, sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Was wir bieten
30 Urlaubstage
Aktives Gesundheitsmanagement
Aus- und Weiterbildung
Firmenhandy
Firmenlaptop
Unfallversicherung
Sport- und Fitnessangebote
Parkplatz
Mobile Arbeit
Mitarbeiterevents
Mitarbeiterprämien
Mitarbeiterrabatte
Kantine
Kinderbetreuung
Internetnutzung
Hunde geduldet
Flexible Arbeitszeiten
Gute Verkehrsanbindung
Gewinnbeteiligung
Wir freuen uns, Dich kennenzulernen.
Bewirb Dich online in unserem Bewerberportal.
Marie Kubitz
HR Services
Tel: +49 5401 39 - 5128

description:
Unna | Verwaltung
Vollzeit
Ausbildung/Trainee
Dein Profil
Du begeisterst Dich für neue IT-Technologien und möchtest deswegen gerne im Bereich der Softwareentwicklung arbeiten? Denn dafür brauchen wir Dich! Was wir gut finden würden?

Du hast erste Erfahrungen z.B. durch die Erstellung einer Website oder mit OpenSource Projekten gesammelt
Du besitzt gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Du bringst Begeisterung für IT-Systeme, Digitalisierung und Software-Entwicklung mit
Du interessierst Dich für logische Zusammenhänge und Technologien
Du bringst Qualitätsbewusstsein, Genauigkeit und eine große Portion Motivation mit
Deine Aufgaben
Was wirst Du bei uns lernen? Du arbeitest mit den anderen aus Deinem Team an einem der besten Produkte zur Digitalisierung von Geschäfts- und Dokumentenprozessen und erhältst ein besonderes Knowhow durch verschiedene Bereiche:

Mit- und Weiterentwicklung der von uns programmierten Software zur Digitalisierung von Prozessen
Forschung in den Bereichen der künstlichen Intelligenz, Machine Learning & Computer Vision
Konzeption, Entwicklung und Realisierung von eigenen Softwareprojekten
Agile Software-Entwicklung
Fehlererkennung und -behebung mithilfe von Experten-, und Diagnosesystemen
Präsentation Deiner entwickelten Anwendungen
Warum wir?
Wir setzen auf die gemeinsame, dynamische Weiterentwicklung unserer innovativen Produkte und glauben an Lösungen und Chancen, nicht an Probleme. Wir sind überzeugt, dass wir uns mit den geballten Kräften unseres Teams, mit Transparenz und viel Spaß an der Arbeit gegenseitig motivieren, herausfordern und täglich zu neuen Höchstleistungen pushen. Werde Teil unseres großartigen Teams, bewirb Dich auf unserer Website und starte im Herbst 2026 Deine Ausbildung bei uns!
Über uns
Die M.S.E. GmbH perfektioniert die Kombination aus exzellent auf den Kunden abgestimmten Hardware-Lösungen mit intelligenter, cloudbasierter Software-Technologie. Unser Ziel ist es, betriebliche Prozesse zu optimieren, maximal zu vereinfachen und zu automatisieren. Kurz gesagt – Wir machen den Menschen das Leben leichter!

description:
We are seeking a dedicated Senior Database Administrator to support the design, implementation, and ongoing management of database environments. In this role, you will be responsible for ensuring the performance, scalability, and reliability of critical systems through effective administration, monitoring, and optimization. You will collaborate closely with application development teams, contribute to key operational processes, and support deployments across on-premises and cloud environments. This position requires a proactive approach to problem-solving, a strong focus on automation and efficiency, and a commitment to maintaining high standards in security, compliance, and service delivery.
Support the design and implementation of relational and NoSQL databases.
Perform core database administration tasks, including upgrades, patching, tuning, backups, troubleshooting, and support.
Collaborate with application development teams to test, optimize, and troubleshoot database-driven applications.
Contribute to daily operational activities impacting key IT processes and systems.
Develop scripts and utilities to automate repetitive database tasks (non-development role).

Monitor database environments (production, test, development) to ensure performance, scalability, and availability.

Support deployment of Verisk products across on-premises and cloud environments.
Assist with enterprise security, auditing, and compliance initiatives.
Mentor junior DBAs on best practices and emerging trends.
Maintain professional communication with all collaborations.
Participate in on-call rotation and occasional off-hours support to meet SLAs.
Stay current with industry trends through continuous learning and professional development.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience.
5+ years of database administration experience across multiple DBMS platforms.
Strong experience with cloud databases, particularly AWS RDS, and 24/7 high-availability environments.
Hands-on expertise with databases such as PostgreSQL, MongoDB , MySQL, SQL Server, Oracle, DynamoDB, or Elastic.
Proven ability to troubleshoot database issues (performance, integrity, replication, connectivity, security, etc.).
Solid knowledge of backup, recovery, performance tuning, and overall database health management.
Experience implementing high availability solutions (clustering, replication).
Proficiency with enterprise database tools and Unix/Linux environments (scripting is a plus).
Strong communication, mentoring, and problem-solving skills.
Fluent English (written and spoken).

#LI-AA1

#LI-Remote
Our People, Our Culture

For more than 50 years, Verisk has helped property and casualty insurers make smarter decisions about risk through AI-powered risk modeling, advanced analytics, and technology solutions spanning the entire policy lifecycle. We are a leading strategic data, analytics, and technology partner to the global insurance industry, guided by core values of learning, caring, and results while maintaining the highest ethical standards as stewards of the industry's most comprehensive datasets. Learn more about Verisk and what we are doing within the insurance industry.

For the eighth consecutive year, Verisk is proudly recognized as a Great Place to Work® for outstanding workplace culture in the US, the fourth consecutive year in the UK, Spain, and India, and the second consecutive year in Poland. In addition, we’ve been recognized by The Wall Street Journal as one of the Best-Managed Companies and by Forbes as a World’s Best Employer, testaments to the value we place on workplace culture.

Our Culture : Explore our inclusive, people-first culture that fosters innovation, collaboration, and belonging.

Awards & Recognition : See why Verisk is consistently recognized as a Great Place to Work™ around the world.

Our Businesses : Learn about the diverse industries we serve — from insurance and energy to financial services and beyond.

Life at Verisk : Discover what it’s like to work at Verisk through employee stories, team highlights, and culture moments.

Careers at Verisk : Join a global team of problem-solvers and innovators doing meaningful work that’s shaping the future of industries. Whether you're just starting out or looking to take your career to the next level, Verisk offers growth, purpose, and a people-first culture

Let’s build something meaningful together!

Verisk Analytics is an equal opportunity employer.

All members of the Verisk Analytics family of companies are equal opportunity employers. We consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, citizenship, sex, gender identity and/or expression, sexual orientation, veteran's status, age or disability. Verisk’s minimum hiring age is 18 except in countries with a higher age limit subject to applicable law.

https://www.verisk.com/company/careers/

Unsolicited resumes sent to Verisk, including unsolicited resumes sent to a Verisk business mailing address, fax machine, or email address, or directly to Verisk employees, will be considered Verisk property. Verisk will NOT pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume.

Verisk Employee Privacy Notice
